mnemonics: Covariance is like a row of numbers tied together (Cov(X,Y) = E[(X-E(X))(Y-E(Y))]
Correlation is like a row of numbers in a matching game (Corr(X,Y) = Cov(X,Y)/(σX*σY)
keyPoints: Covariance measures how much two variables change together
Correlation measures the strength and direction of the linear relationship between two variables
Covariance and correlation are measures of association, not causation
